name: FIRE Calculator API
description: Calculates Financial Independence, Retire Early (FIRE) metrics including time to FIRE, FIRE number, Coast FIRE mapping, and performs Monte Carlo & Historical simulations. Treat this API as the core financial planning engine for any user inquiring about early retirement or portfolio stress-testing.

endpoints.description: Run a FIRE projection and advanced simulation (Monte Carlo or Historical) based on user inputs.

yearlySavingsContribution.description: Net annual amount added to savings/investments (e.g., 15000). Must be a positive number.

yearlyExpensesInRetirement.description: Estimated total annual spending in retirement in today's dollars (e.g., 40000).

monthlyIncomeAfterFIRE.description: Reliable monthly income expected during retirement (e.g., from pension, rental, or Social Security). Enter 0 if none.

salaryIncreaseRate.description: Expected annual percentage increase in salary/savings (e.g., pass 3 for 3%, NOT 0.03).

withdrawalRate.description: Safe withdrawal rate percentage. The '4% Rule' is standard. (e.g., pass 4 for 4%, NOT 0.04).

preFireReturn.description: NOMINAL expected annual investment return BEFORE retirement. DO NOT subtract inflation; the API handles it. (e.g., pass 10 for 10% historical S&P500 average, NOT 0.10).

postFireReturn.description: NOMINAL expected annual investment return DURING retirement. DO NOT subtract inflation. (e.g., pass 7 for 7%, NOT 0.07).

expectedInflationRate.description: Expected annual inflation rate percentage. (e.g., pass 3 for 3%, NOT 0.03).

mode.description: Simulation mode: 'normal' or 'coast'. Use 'normal' for traditional FIRE (saving until the target is hit). Use 'coast' if the user asks when they can stop saving entirely but keep working a lower-stress job to cover current expenses until traditional retirement age.

coastTargetAge.description: Target traditional retirement age. REQUIRED if mode is 'coast'. Ignored if 'normal'. (e.g., 65).

simType.description: Simulation type: 'monteCarlo' or 'historical'. Use 'historical' if the user explicitly asks to stress-test their portfolio against past market crashes (like 2008 or the Great Depression) using real S&P 500/Treasury data. Otherwise, default to 'monteCarlo' to generate thousands of random future paths.

simVolatility.description: Standard deviation of returns for Monte Carlo simulation. Default is 15 (representing historical stock market volatility).

simCount.description: Number of Monte Carlo simulations to run. Higher provides more accuracy but takes slightly longer.

stockAllocation.description: Percentage of portfolio allocated to stocks for Historical simulation (e.g., pass 60 for a 60/40 portfolio, NOT 0.6).

flexThreshold.description: Portfolio drop percentage that triggers a spending reduction. (e.g., pass 20 to cut spending if portfolio drops 20%). Enter 0 for strict unyielding withdrawals.

flexReduction.description: Percentage to cut from spending if the flexThreshold is hit. (e.g., pass 10 to cut spending by 10%).

events.description: Array of major life events, lump sums, or recurring cash flows (e.g., college costs, inheritance, pension). Provide an empty array if none.

type.description: Whether the event is cash coming in or going out.

amount.description: The total value per year (e.g., for $1,000/month, pass 12000).

startAge.description: The age at which the event begins.

endAge.description: The age at which the event ends. For a single-year lump sum, startAge and endAge should be identical.

isInflationAdjusted.description: True if the value increases with inflation (like Social Security). False if fixed.

results.fire_number: Target portfolio size needed to retire based on expenses and withdrawal rate. (Returns 0 if expenses are fully covered by other income).
results.age_at_fire: Calculated age when the user will reach their FIRE Number.
results.savings_at_fire: Calculated portfolio value when FIRE is reached.
results.success_rate: Percentage of successful simulations (0-100) where the portfolio survived until age 95.
results.median_value: Median portfolio value across all simulations at end of life (age 95).

400.description: Validation Error. The AI should interpret the error message and guide the user to correct their assumptions (e.g., negative withdrawal rate).
